title: Cymatic Planner FAIL: no plan from flat to hexagons within bound k=1 (requires k=2)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates planner failure with documented obstruction: max_depth_k=1 is insufficient to reach hexagons from flat. BFS explores flat (depth 0) and expands to stripes (depth 1); stripes != hexagons, and depth limit is reached. Planner returns found_plan=false with NO_PLAN_WITHIN_BOUND + GOAL_NOT_REACHABLE. This is the negative fixture proving the planner is not cheating: it correctly refuses to emit a plan when the bound is too tight. QA analogue: attempting to certify a singularity->cosmos reachability witness in a single T-step — which is algebraically impossible because the satellite threshold must be crossed first.

qa_mapping.planning_qa_analogue: max_depth_k=1 cannot bridge singularity->cosmos. BFS from flat expands to stripes (satellite orbit) at depth 1 but the goal hexagons (cosmos) is at depth 2 minimum. The satellite-threshold crossing is structurally mandatory — no edge in the control graph skips it. QA analogue: the Q(√5) norm separates orbit families; a single T-application on the fixed-point state (b=9,e=9 mod 9) cannot produce a cosmos-orbit pair in one step.

validation_checks.details: bfs in {bfs, dfs, astar, dijkstra, iddfs}

validation_checks.description: path_length_k <= max_depth_k (vacuous when no plan found)
every plan step is a legal edge in control_graph
validation_checks.details: steps=[]; vacuously true — no steps to check

validation_checks.description: observed final pattern class matches target pattern class
validation_checks.details: flat != hexagons — no plan found, system remains at initial state

validation_checks.description: BFS recomputed shortest path within bound also absent (planner not cheating)
validation_checks.details: BFS recomputes: no path flat->hexagons within max_depth_k=1; consistent with found_plan=false

validation_checks.description: no disordered/out_of_orbit state visited in plan

fail_ledger.message: BFS exhausted all paths reachable from 'flat' within max_depth_k=1 without reaching 'hexagons'. The only depth-1 neighbor of flat is 'stripes' (via increase_amplitude), and stripes is not the target. The minimal path flat->stripes->hexagons requires k=2 moves. Increase max_depth_k to at least 2 to find a plan.

Target pattern 'hexagons' (QA orbit: cosmos) is not reachable from 'flat' (QA orbit: singularity) within the declared bound k=1. The legal control graph requires passing through 'stripes' (satellite orbit) as a mandatory intermediate — this threshold crossing cannot be skipped, and consumes one of the k steps. QA analogue: singularity->cosmos reachability requires at least two generator applications (satellite threshold crossing is structurally mandatory).
